sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- White pixel at [24, 15] - on the yellow cross now!
- Blue cross at cols 35-61 (centered at col 48)
- Yellow cross at [24, 15] with full structure
- 3 yellow dots remaining: [3, 15], [9, 6], [9, 24]
So when I moved the blue cross LEFT to overlap with the yellow cross, the white pixel switched to the yellow cross! The blue cross stayed at its position (cols 35-61), and the yellow cross is now at [24, 15] with the white pixel.
Now I can control the yellow cross! The yellow dots are:
- [3, 15]: row 3, col 15
- [9, 6]: row 9, col 6
- [9, 24]: row 9, col 24
The yellow cross is at [24, 15]. Its horizontal bar is at row 24, vertical bar at col 15.
To collect [9, 6] and [9, 24], I need to move the yellow cross UP so the horizontal bar is at row 9. That's (24-9)/3 = 5 UP moves.
After 5 UPs, the yellow cross center would be at [9, 15], with horizontal bar at row 9 spanning cols 15-11=4 to 15+11=26. This would cover [9, 6] (col 6) and [9, 24] (col 24).
Then to collect [3, 15], I need to move UP more so the horizontal bar is at row 3. From row 9, that's (9-3)/3 = 2 more UP moves. After 2 more UPs, center at [3, 15], horizontal bar at row 3.
So the plan is: 5 UP to collect [9, 6] and [9, 24], then 2 more UP to collect [3, 15]. Total: 7 UP moves.
Let me try this!

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move UP 7 times to collect all 3 yellow dots
# First 5 UPs: center goes from [24, 15] to [9, 15], horizontal bar at row 9 covers [9, 6] and [9, 24]
# Then 2 more UPs: center goes from [9, 15] to [3, 15], horizontal bar at row 3 covers [3, 15]
action(['UP'] * 7)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
